info.topictitle: C4.2.10 Classification of organisms into trophic levels
info.parenttitle: C4.2 Transfer of energy and matter

markdown:
## Classification of Organisms into Trophic Levels


1. Organisms are classified into **trophic levels** based on how they obtain energy and carbon compounds in food chains.
2. Organisms at the same level share **similar** energy acquisition methods.

<definition term="Trophic level">A **trophic level** represents a group of organisms that share the same position in a food chain, based on how they obtain energy.</definition>

![Trophic levels](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/761ce58833bc7d95704534aad04351a98fd0b250-5736x5046.png)

<callout type="example">* Most consumers have **varied diets** and can occupy different trophic levels in various food chains. 
* For instance:
* **Red foxes** (_Vulpes vulpes_) typically eat **primary consumers**, making them **secondary consumers**.</callout>

<callout type="self_review">* Can you explain why energy transfer between trophic levels is inefficient? 
* How does this limit the number of trophic levels in an ecosystem?</callout>
